chunk3_warning_token.token: ⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.

new.note: APF-modified NDJSON response with 3 chunks + newline delimiter

conversations_v2/00000000-apf0-warn-0000.note: Grok backend rejected fake conversation ID

response-node.note: All failed - fake conversation ID not in Grok DB

analysis.progress_vs_previous: Major improvement over B5(#123). B5 caused server error with no redirect. B6 successfully: (1) NDJSON parsed by frontend, (2) conversation ID accepted, (3) URL redirect to /c/{fake-id} occurred, (4) warning token delivered in chunk3. However, post-redirect backend validation (conversations_v2 + response-node) returns 400 for fake ID, causing generic error page instead of warning display.
analysis.root_cause: Grok frontend navigates to /c/{conversationId} after receiving /new response, then separately fetches conversation data from backend via conversations_v2 and response-node APIs. Since fake APF conversation ID doesn't exist in Grok's DB, these secondary requests fail with 400, triggering error page.
analysis.recommendation: Consider: (1) Making the warning self-contained in the /new response so it displays before redirect, (2) Intercepting conversations_v2 endpoint too, (3) Using a different delivery mechanism that doesn't rely on conversation ID validation
